column1: 50 Hz actually produced MORE voltage than 25 Hz.
That is very typical for vibration harvesters — many are tuned around 40-60 Hz.
So the device is behaving like a real resonant harvester
That is over twice the slope of your 25 Hz test.
50 Hz charges the capacitor more than twice as fast.
Even though industrial machines often run at 25 Hz or 30 Hz.
This indicates the capacitor continued charging strongly near peak output.
The device likely would exceed 1.1 V if the excitation continued longer.
This is a very good sign.
